title: Fixtures, Golden Files, Properties, Fuzzing, HTML Validation, and Browser Tests · Program Brief: Fuzz Resource
summary: Tests snapshot everything, approve changed HTML blindly, omit malformed input, compare only strings, never mutate fixtures, and treat validator success as user-flow proof. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fuzz resource increment in layered parser-to-browser verification matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Fuzz malformed delimiters, nesting, Unicode, metadata, paths, templates, and assets under time, memory, depth, file, and diagnostic budgets.
Check consumption, round-trip boundaries, tree validity, escaping, route uniqueness, determinism, clean equivalence, and idempotence across generated cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-fuzz-resource — Fuzz malformed delimiters, nesting, Unicode, metadata, paths, templates, and assets under time, memory, depth, file, and diagnostic budgets.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-testing-validation-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-testing-validation-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_testing_validation_4(fixture_cases: int = 48, detected_mutants: int = 46, required_mutants: int = 45, validator_errors: int = 0, browser_tasks_failed: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0:
        return False, detected_mutants, "test-shape"
    if detected_mutants < required_mutants:
        return False, detected_mutants, "mutation-gap"
    if validator_errors > 0:
        return False, validator_errors, "invalid-output"
    if browser_tasks_failed > 0:
        return False, browser_tasks_failed, "reader-flow-failure"
    assert detected_mutants >= required_mutants
    return True, detected_mutants, "layered-verification"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-fuzz-resource-1u6p7w
# Competency: ssg-fuzz-resource.
# Case 6691: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_fuzz_resource_1u6p7w(fixture_cases: int = 48, detected_mutants: int = 46, required_mutants: int = 45, validator_errors: int = 0, browser_tasks_failed: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1u6p7w = "1u6p7w-w7p6u1-6691"
    assert evidence_variant_1u6p7w
    evidence_axes_1u6p7w = (
        "reader1u6p7w",
        "source6691",
        "parsew7p6u1",
        "route1u6p7w6691",
        "artifact66911u6p7w",
        "recoveryw7p6u16691",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1u6p7w)) == 6
    if fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0:
        return False, detected_mutants, "test-shape"
    if detected_mutants < required_mutants:
        return False, detected_mutants, "mutation-gap"
    if validator_errors > 0:
        return False, validator_errors, "invalid-output"
    if browser_tasks_failed > 0:
        return False, browser_tasks_failed, "reader-flow-failure"
    assert detected_mutants >= required_mutants
    return True, detected_mutants, "layered-verification"

At Static-site publishing and Python Build Fuzz Resource evidence · Theory Fuzz Resource, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 6483 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Fuzz malformed delimiters, nesting, Unicode, metadata, paths, templates, and assets under time, memory, depth, file, and diagnostic budgets.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Fuzz Resource model · Theory Fuzz Resource
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Fuzz Resource: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 4864 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Fuzz malformed delimiters, nesting, Unicode, metadata, paths, templates, and assets under time, memory, depth, file, and diagnostic budgets.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Fuzz Resource model · Theory Fuzz Resource.
steps.why: Retrieving Fuzz Resource through the concrete layered parser-to-browser verification matrix case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Fuzz Resource model · Theory Fuzz Resource, keep Fuzz Resource connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
